entities.name: The Siam Cement Public Company Limited (SCG)

financial.note: Holds ~33% stake; ensures long-term institutional stability and alignment with national infrastructure interests.
Major providers of long-term debt and debenture management; SCC's ฿30bn 2026 investment plan is sensitive to bank credit terms and policy rates.

supply_chain.note: Primary profit driver; earnings are highly sensitive to the PE/PP-Naphtha spread, which is projected to recover in 2H26 as global oversupply eases.
A ฿16bn Ethane Feedstock enhancement project (2026-2027) aims to lower production costs and capture Vietnam's 7% GDP growth.
Provides defensive cash flow (~75% consumer-linked); 2026 focus on mergers and partnerships in Vietnam and Indonesia.
Energy and feedstock costs directly impact margins for the Cement and Chemical units; price spikes are a major downside risk.

equity_cross_holdings.note: Primary distribution channel for SCC's cement and building products in regional Thailand.

competitors.note: Direct rival in the domestic cement and construction materials market.
Competes in the olefins and polymers sector; rival for global petrochemical market share.

macro_commodity_factors.logic: The single most important driver for SCC's bottom line; consensus expects a rebound from early 2026 troughs by 3Q26.
Drives volume for the Cement-Construction Materials business (CBM); 2026 public project acceleration is a key catalyst.
A weaker THB boosts translated export revenue (Chemicals/Packaging) but increases the cost of imported coal and naphtha.
Impacts the cost of servicing the ฿400bn+ asset base and influences the valuation of its diverse investment portfolio.
